claim: AI agents are only as useful as the context you provide them; building a proper context infrastructure is fundamental for agent success
actionable: Invest time early in establishing a robust context layer for your agents—use a second brain/operating system approach with centralized context files rather than embedding context in each chat.
source_title: The 7 Levels of Using Claude Context Explained in 24 min

claim: Claude Projects solve the manual copy-paste context problem for repetitive tasks but are limited to isolated chat windows
actionable: Move beyond Chat Projects to Skills (in Claude Code/Cowork) for cross-chat reusability and better automation.
source_title: The 7 Levels of Using Claude Context Explained in 24 min

claim: Skills are superior to Chat Projects because they live outside isolated chat windows and can be triggered from any conversation
actionable: Build Skills (using skill.md + references folder) and share them via zip files or organizational skills in Cowork for reuse across team.
source_title: The 7 Levels of Using Claude Context Explained in 24 min

claim: Use Obsidian as a free tool to visualize and organize your context folder structure when managing a large personal operating system
actionable: Download Obsidian (obsidian.md) and point it to your context folder to visualize relationships and organize files at scale.
source_title: The 7 Levels of Using Claude Context Explained in 24 min

claim: The Claude.md file is critical for routing agents to the right context; it becomes more important as your context vault grows
actionable: Create a Claude.md file that documents folder structure, Obsidian syntax, wiki links, and rules for navigating your context.
source_title: The 7 Levels of Using Claude Context Explained in 24 min

claim: Use scheduled tasks to automatically update your second brain with real-time context (meeting transcripts, task lists, analytics)
actionable: Set up scheduled tasks (e.g., via Firefly connector) to auto-update your OS folder with daily meeting transcripts and task roll-ups.
source_title: The 7 Levels of Using Claude Context Explained in 24 min

claim: Use Relay (Obsidian community plugin) or custom solutions to sync context files across your team in real-time
actionable: Install Relay in Obsidian settings > community plugins to sync folders to team members; use Ben AI's custom plugin for permission controls.
source_title: The 7 Levels of Using Claude Context Explained in 24 min

claim: When rolling out agentic OS to a team, designate one person as operator/manager to maintain context layer—it requires ongoing maintenance
actionable: Assign clear ownership for weekly context maintenance and dedicate time to remove duplicates, resolve conflicts, and reorganize files.
source_title: The 7 Levels of Using Claude Context Explained in 24 min
